subtitle: A working, red-flagged beach north of Liverpool where 100 life-size cast-iron men by Antony Gormley — each a cast of his own body — stand across two miles of tidal sand, submerged and uncovered by every tide.
spike_statement: One hundred cast-iron men, each a cast of Antony Gormley's body, stand across two miles of tidal sand at Crosby, drowned and revealed by every tide.

levers.photo_tone: rust-brown cast-iron figures on flat wet gray tidal sand facing a pale Liverpool Bay, some half-submerged, barnacle-crusted and corroding, container ships on the horizon
